incorrects.description: Student selected 16: counted symbols instead of skip-counting by scale

params.description: Key pulses to draw attention to scale.

steps.text: You counted the symbols. But each symbol stands for 5 books, not 1. Look at the key. Skip-count by 5s.

incorrects.description: Student selected 25: miscounted or added an extra skip-count

params.description: Symbols pulse one at a time to support counting.

steps.text: Count the symbols first. There are 4. Now skip-count by 5s: 5, 10, 15... What comes next?

incorrects.description: Student selected 15: stopped one skip-count short

steps.text: You skip-counted 5, 10, 15. But there are 4 symbols. Count all 4: 5, 10, 15, and one more.

incorrects.description: Student answered incorrectly. System models the correct answer.

params.description: Symbols highlight one at a time as guide counts: 5, 10, 15, 20.

steps.text: Let me show you. I count the symbols: 1, 2, 3, 4. Each symbol means 5 books. So I skip-count: 5, 10, 15, 20. The answer is 20 books. That's how we use the key to read scaled picture graphs.
